mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 2.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances despite dual demand contraction, or interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in industrial production below 107.5 signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, which constrains profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO < 107.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate above 4.2% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.2%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 252.0) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) indicates sustained monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200 thousand units signals strong residential investment demand and forward-looking economic optimism, which supports equity valuations through higher expected GDP growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: demand-side expansion or housing cycle recovery
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when HOUST > 1,200 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence that sustains corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both series show quarter-over-quarter growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 0% AND RSAFS monthly growth > 0%
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumption and output
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus single-series models.
